title: Extend fabric-hint.cjs to capture `KB:` first-line + emit `assistant_turn_observed` per turn
description: Extend summarizeTranscript to add a `role:'assistant'` branch that harvests the first non-empty text line from the first text block of each assistant envelope, applies regex /^KB:\s+(.+)$/ or /^KB:\s*none$/, and accumulates one assistant_turn_observed payload per envelope (turn_id = session_id + envelope_index). Insert new helper `extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort` at L1134 right after writeSessionDigestBestEffort, wrapping inline JSON.stringify emit (node_modules unavailable). Honors three-end shape variance: Claude Code transcript path verified; Codex assume-and-test; Cursor silent skip (rc.21 scope).

files.target: summarizeTranscript (L1011-L1088) + new helper after writeSessionDigestBestEffort (L1112) + insertion call at L1134 + constants block (L28-L29)
files.change: (1) Add `EVENT_TYPE_ASSISTANT_TURN_OBSERVED = 'assistant_turn_observed'` constant near existing EVENT_TYPE_* literals at L28-L31. (2) Extend summarizeTranscript: when envelope.role === 'assistant', iterate content blocks; for the FIRST text block only, take its first non-empty line; apply regex `/^KB:\s+(.+)$/` or `/^KB:\s*none$/i`; if match, parse cite_ids + cite_tags via tag-enum splitter (planned|recalled|chained-from <id>|dismissed:<reason>|none); push {turn_id: sessionId+'-'+envelopeIndex, kb_line_raw, cite_ids, cite_tags, envelope_index, ts} to assistantTurns[]. (3) Add `parseKbLine(raw)` helper that returns {cite_ids, cite_tags}: splits multi-cite lines (comma-separated KB: a, b, c [tag][tag]) per spec, handles dismissed:<reason> embedded reason. (4) Add `client` detection helper: read process.env.FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT (set by hook config — 'cc' / 'codex' / 'cursor') OR infer from path heuristic (.claude/hooks vs .codex/hooks vs .cursor/hooks in __dirname). (5) Add new `extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort(cwd, stdinPayload)` after L1112: re-uses parsed transcript output, iterates assistantTurns[], appends one event line per turn to .fabric/events.jsonl with inline JSON.stringify; never throws (outer try/catch). (6) Insert call at L1134 right after `writeSessionDigestBestEffort(cwd, payload)`.

files.target: Stop hook command env block (around L3-L13)
files.change: Set FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT=cc env var on the Stop hook command (so fabric-hint.cjs can label events with client='cc' without path-heuristic ambiguity). If config schema doesn't support env, fall back to path heuristic only — record decision in TASK-03 commit.
Set FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT=codex similarly (or document path-heuristic fallback).

implementation: Read packages/cli/templates/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs L987-L1112 to confirm tryReadStdinJson + summarizeTranscript + writeSessionDigestBestEffort shapes.
Define EVENT_TYPE_ASSISTANT_TURN_OBSERVED constant string near L28-L31.

Implement parseKbLine(raw) helper: input = raw string after `KB:` prefix; output = {cite_ids:string[], cite_tags:string[]}. Handle: `none` → cite_ids=[], cite_tags=['none']. Multi-cite: `KP-001, KT-DEC-0009 (review) [recalled][chained-from KP-002]` → parse comma-separated ids + bracket-extracted tag enum (split on `]` `[`).

Extend summarizeTranscript: add `assistantTurns = []` accumulator; on envelope.role === 'assistant', find first content block of type 'text'; take first non-empty line of block.text; apply regex; if match, push entry; envelope_index = position in transcript ordered.

Detect client: prefer process.env.FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT; fallback to __dirname.includes('.claude/') ? 'cc' : __dirname.includes('.codex/') ? 'codex' : __dirname.includes('.cursor/') ? 'cursor' : undefined.

Implement extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort(cwd, stdinPayload): wrap in try/catch; if stdinPayload?.transcript_path falsy → return; call summarizeTranscript (already returns assistantTurns[] via extended return shape); for each turn, build event object with kind:'fabric-event' + id (random uuid-ish using Date.now() + Math.random() per existing pattern) + ts (Date.now()) + schema_version:1 + session_id (stdinPayload.session_id) + event_type + kb_line_raw + cite_ids + cite_tags + client + turn_id + envelope_index + timestamp (new Date().toISOString()); append JSON.stringify(event) + '\n' to .fabric/events.jsonl using fs.appendFileSync.
Insert helper call at L1134 right after writeSessionDigestBestEffort(cwd, payload) — same indent, same try-wrap (helper is internally best-effort).
Update hook config files to set FABRIC_HINT_CLIENT env var per client (cc/codex).

reference.pattern: Hook-side inline emit (constant duplication + JSON.stringify + appendFileSync)
reference.examples: writeSessionDigestBestEffort is the structural twin: best-effort wrapper, reuses stdinPayload, never throws.

rationale.chosen_approach: Extend summarizeTranscript in-place to return both user/edit data AND assistant-turn data in one pass — single JSONL parse cost. Inline emit (no module import) preserves node_modules-unavailable invariant.
alternatives_considered: Sibling parser function reading transcript second time (rejected: doubles I/O)
Lib module under packages/cli/templates/hooks/lib/cite-parser.cjs (rejected: KT-DEC-0009 single-script preference unless parser grows >50 lines)
Defer client field to TASK-04 (rejected: hook is the only authoritative source for client identity)
rationale.tradeoffs: summarizeTranscript return shape grows (now returns {user_messages, edit_paths, assistant_turns}); minor refactor risk for existing callers — mitigated by additive-only field.

manual_checks: Manually invoke fabric-hint.cjs with a fixture stdin payload + a mock transcript JSONL containing assistant envelopes with KB: lines; verify events.jsonl gets new lines
Run fab install in a test workspace, simulate a Claude Code session, confirm .fabric/events.jsonl contains assistant_turn_observed events with correct cite_tags parsing
Test KB: none line → event has cite_tags:['none'], cite_ids:[]
success_metrics: assistant_turn_observed events appear in .fabric/events.jsonl after Stop hook fires
Multi-cite lines correctly split into multiple cite_ids
Hook never blocks: artificial parse error → silent exit 0
unit: fabric-hint.test extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort silent on missing transcript
integration: Manual: fab install in test repo → simulate Claude Code Stop hook with fixture transcript → assert events.jsonl content

criteria: summarizeTranscript returns assistant_turns[] array in addition to existing fields
parseKbLine helper correctly parses all 4 tag enum values + multi-cite + dismissed reason embedding + KB: none
extractAndWriteAssistantTurnsBestEffort called at L1134 immediately after writeSessionDigestBestEffort
Hook never throws: fixture test with malformed stdin payload exits 0 silently
Generated event lines match Zod schema from TASK-02 (parseable by appendEventLedgerEvent)
client field populated correctly: 'cc' for Claude Code path, 'codex' for Codex path, undefined for Cursor (rc.21 deferred)
Commit msg: `feat(rc20): fabric-hint captures KB cite line + emits turn event (TASK-03)`
